questions.question: Explain the working principle of Selection Sort with an example.
keyPoints: Find minimum element from unsorted portion
Swap with first position of unsorted portion

questions.question: Derive the time complexity of Bubble Sort in best, average and worst cases.
keyPoints: Best case: O(n) - already sorted with optimization

questions.question: Why is Selection Sort considered unstable? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Shows how equal elements may change positions

questions.question: Write the algorithm for one pass of Bubble Sort and explain how it moves the largest element to its correct position.
keyPoints: After n-1 comparisons, largest reaches end

questions.question: Compare Selection Sort and Bubble Sort based on (i) Number of swaps (ii) Stability (iii) Adaptive property.
keyPoints: Swaps: Selection = n-1 (minimum), Bubble = variable (0 to O(n²))
Stability: Selection = unstable, Bubble = can be stable
Adaptive: Selection = not adaptive, Bubble = can be (with flag)

questions.question: What is the advantage of adding a flag variable in Bubble Sort? Explain with a case.
keyPoints: Flag detects if any swaps occurred
If no swaps, array is sorted
Best case improves from O(n²) to O(n)

questions.question: Trace through Selection Sort for the array: [64, 25, 12, 22, 11] showing the array after each pass.
